name: Locust (Python Load Testing Tool)

auth.notes: Simulates user auth patterns; no external auth needed

performance.notes: Generates load; single machine can simulate thousands of concurrent users

agent_readiness.notes: Python-based load testing with web UI. Define user behavior as Python classes inheriting User. @task decorator for weighted task selection. HttpUser, FastHttpUser (geventhttpclient, 5-6x faster). Distributed mode for multiple load generators. Real-time web UI with charts, statistics, failures. CSV export. Custom shapes (ramp-up patterns). Events system for custom reporting. Alternative to k6 (JS) and Gatling (Scala). More Pythonic and hackable than k6. wait_time: between_tasks, constant, constant_pacing.

gotchas: GIL limits concurrency on CPython — use gevent/FastHttpUser
Less performant than k6 or Gatling for extreme loads
Distributed mode requires manual coordination of workers
No built-in protocol support beyond HTTP (plugins exist)
best_when: Python teams wanting programmable load tests
Teams needing hackable, custom load patterns in Python
Development environment load testing with web UI
avoid_when: Very high load requirements (>10k RPS, use k6 or Gatling)
Teams preferring JavaScript for all tooling (use k6)
summary: Locust is a Python load testing tool where user behavior is defined as Python classes with @task decorators. Web UI with real-time charts. FastHttpUser for high-throughput. Distributed mode for scaling across machines. Most Pythonic load testing option — easy to customize and integrate with Python CI. For extreme load or JavaScript teams, k6 or Gatling offer better raw performance.
